channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Central African Republic: Foreign Aid Priorities Fail to Support Key Development Sectors
item.description: The Central African Republic (CAR) is among the world's least developed countries with very slow progress. Many people used to call it an 'aid orphan,' but researchers found that foreign aid has actua...

item.title: The Global Health and Environmental Impact of Plastic Pollution
item.description: Plastic pollution has become a pervasive global problem, with microplastics now detected in human blood, breast milk, heart arteries, lungs, testicles, brains, and placentas. These tiny particles, sma...

item.title: Family of Pune child rape-murder accused disowns him as outrage grows and SIT probe begins
item.description: A deeply disturbing incident in Pune has triggered widespread public outrage after a three-and-a-half-year-old girl was allegedly raped and murdered by a 65-year-old labourer, Bhimrao Kamble, on May 1...

item.title: Cybercriminal Forums Frustrated by Low-Quality AI Content
item.description: Cybercriminals and low-level hackers are increasingly frustrated by the presence of AI-generated content on forums where they discuss illegal activities. A study analyzing nearly 98,000 AI-related pos...

item.title: Kenya Power Schedules Electricity Maintenance in Nairobi, Nyeri, Kiambu, and Embu Counties
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has informed the public that some areas in Nairobi, Nyeri, Kiambu, and Embu will face electricity interruptions on Thursday, May 7. This is part of scheduled sy...

item.title: Thousands gather in Durban CBD to protest against undocumented migrants and alleged crime
item.description: On 6 May 2026, thousands of protesters took to the Durban CBD, aiming to highlight illegal activity and target undocumented foreigners. The demonstrators, many of them Zulus dressed in traditional att...

item.title: Four Farmers Die in Telangana After Sheds Collapse During Heavy Rain
item.description: In Telangana’s Mancherial district, four farmers tragically lost their lives, and three others sustained injuries when tin-roofed sheds collapsed in two separate incidents. The accidents occurred late...

item.title: Michelin 2026 Quebec Guide Recognizes New Restaurants with Stars
item.description: The 2026 Michelin guide for Quebec, the second edition of the provincial guide, has been released, awarding stars to four new restaurants. The newly recognised restaurants, each receiving one star, in...

item.title: Barrie Colts fined $15,000 by OHL for inappropriate post-game media conduct
item.description: The Barrie Colts of the Ontario Hockey League (OHL) have been fined $15,000 for conduct deemed detrimental to the league's public image. The fine followed a post-game press conference that went viral ...

item.title: Kynpham Sing Nongkynrih Reflects on the Humanitarian Tragedy in Gaza
item.description: Kynpham Sing Nongkynrih, a poet and novelist from Shillong, presents a poignant reflection on the ongoing humanitarian crisis in Gaza through his literary works. He critiques the euphemistic language ...

item.title: Gram Sabhas approving Nicobar project had very low attendance, below legal requirement
item.description: The Andaman and Nicobar Islands administration has come under scrutiny for failing to meet the mandatory quorum in Gram Sabha meetings held for consent to the ₹92,000-crore Great Nicobar Island projec...

item.title: Khalif Kairo opens up on regret over delaying marriage before his mother’s death
item.description: Businessman Khalif Kairo has shared a personal and emotional reflection about his late mother, revealing that one of his biggest regrets is not getting married before she passed away. Speaking in an i...

item.title: Aliko Dangote announces 20,000MW power project to address Nigeria’s electricity shortage
item.description: Aliko Dangote, wey be Africa richest man, don announce say him wan invest for huge 20,000MW power generation project for Nigeria. Dis project dey part of him wider strategy to boost industrial growth ...

item.title: Second Edition of Namma Art Bengaluru 2026 Showcases Over 150 Artists Across India
item.description: The second edition of Namma Art Bengaluru, a national-level art exhibition, commenced on May 6 at Karnataka Chitrakala Parishath, Bengaluru, and will remain open till May 10. This exhibition features ...

item.title: Tanzania Invites Didier Drogba to Support Sports Development Ahead of 2027 AFCON
item.description: Tanzania's President Samia Suluhu Hassan has formally requested Didier Drogba, the famous former Ivory Coast striker and Chelsea star, to serve as the country's special envoy ahead of the 2027 Africa ...

item.title: Challenges Faced by Zoos and Wildlife During Armed Conflicts
item.description: Animals in Ukraine are suffering alongside humans due to the ongoing war, facing threats from shelling, hunger, cold, and displacement. Zoo animals are particularly vulnerable because they rely on hum...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Balances Motherhood and Studies, Graduates as Doctor
item.description: Antonio B, a determined Kenyan woman, has successfully graduated as a doctor despite the enormous challenge of starting university with a six-week-old baby. She shared her journey on social media, exp...

item.title: Health and Safety Measures as MV Hondius Arrives in the Canary Islands
item.description: The MV Hondius, a research vessel, is set to dock in the Canary Islands, prompting health authorities to monitor potential risks closely. This arrival has gained attention due to concerns about infect...

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel Discloses Discovery of Classified Documents in Hidden Storage
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has revealed the existence of a secret room within the bureau containing 'burn bags'—secure containers used to destroy classified information. During a recent podcast appearanc...

item.title: Ted Turner, pioneer of 24-hour news and founder of CNN, dies at 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the visionary media entrepreneur who founded CNN and revolutionised global television news, passed away at the age of 87. According to reports, he had been suffering from Lewy Body Dementi...
